entries.name: Bonjour Gateway Discovery
entries.description: Advertise the local OpenClaw gateway over Bonjour/mDNS.

entries.name: Browser
entries.description: Default browser tool plugin

entries.name: Canvas
entries.description: Experimental Canvas control and A2UI rendering surfaces for paired nodes.

entries.name: Device Pair
entries.description: QR/bootstrap pairing helpers for OpenClaw devices

entries.name: File Transfer
entries.description: Fetch, list, and write files on paired nodes via dedicated node commands.

entries.name: Memory (Core)
entries.description: File-backed memory search tools and CLI

entries.name: Ollama Provider
entries.description: Bundled Ollama provider plugin

entries.name: Phone Control
entries.description: Temporary allowlist control for phone automation commands

entries.name: Talk Voice
entries.description: Command helpers for managing Talk voice configuration

entries.name: Telegram
entries.description: Telegram channel plugin

entries.name: browser-automation
entries.description: Use when controlling web pages with the OpenClaw browser tool, especially multi-step flows, login checks, tab management, or recovery from stale refs/timeouts.

entries.name: canvas
entries.description: Present HTML on connected OpenClaw node canvases, navigate/eval/snapshot, and debug canvas host URLs.

entries.name: clawhub
entries.description: Search ClawHub for skills when a requested capability is not already available; install, verify, update, publish, or sync skills.

entries.name: diagram-maker
entries.description: Create SVG/HTML or Excalidraw diagrams for concepts, architecture, flows, and whiteboards.

entries.name: healthcheck
entries.description: Audit/harden OpenClaw hosts: SSH, firewall, updates, exposure, backups, disk encryption, gateway security.

entries.name: meme-maker
entries.description: Search meme templates, suggest formats, and generate local or hosted image memes.

entries.name: node-connect
entries.description: Diagnose OpenClaw Android, iOS, or macOS node pairing, QR/setup code, route, auth, and connection failures.

entries.name: node-inspect-debugger
entries.description: Debug Node.js with node inspect, --inspect, breakpoints, CDP, heap, and CPU profiles.

entries.name: notion
entries.description: Notion API for creating and managing pages, databases, and blocks.

entries.name: office-docs
entries.description: Generate and manipulate office documents (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX) for professional reports, presentations, and data exports. Use when: (1) Creating PDFs from text/images, (2) Generating Word documents, (3) Creating Excel spreadsheets with formulas, (4) Building PowerPoint presentations, (5) Extracting content from office documents.

entries.name: openclaw-slides
entries.description: Create stunning, animation-rich HTML presentations from scratch or convert PowerPoint files (.ppt/.pptx) to beautiful web slides. Use when the user wants to build a pitch deck, presentation, slideshow, or slide deck — or convert an existing PPT to a web presentation. Generates zero-dependency single HTML files with keyboard/touch navigation and scroll-triggered animations. Style options include Neon Cyber, Bold Signal, Swiss Modern, Paper & Ink, and 8 more curated presets.

entries.name: oracle
entries.description: Use the @steipete/oracle CLI to bundle a prompt plus the right files and get a second-model review (API or browser) for debugging, refactors, design checks, or cross-validation.

entries.name: PDF Generator
entries.description: Generate professional PDFs from Markdown, HTML, data, or code. Reports, invoices, contracts, and documents with best practices.

entries.name: python-debugpy
entries.description: Debug Python with pdb, breakpoint(), post-mortem inspection, and debugpy remote attach.

entries.name: skill-creator
entries.description: Create, edit, audit, tidy, validate, or restructure AgentSkills and SKILL.md files.

entries.name: spike
entries.description: Run throwaway prototypes to validate feasibility, compare approaches, and report a verdict.

entries.name: taskflow
entries.description: Coordinate multi-step detached tasks as one durable TaskFlow job with owner context, state, waits, and child tasks.

entries.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
entries.description: Example TaskFlow pattern for inbox triage, intent routing, waiting on replies, and later summaries.

entries.name: weather
entries.description: Current weather and forecasts with web_fetch, falling back to wttr.in curl for locations, rain, temperature, travel planning.

entries.name: xurl
entries.description: A Twitter research and content intelligence skill focused on attracting WordPress and Shopify clients. Use to analyze Twitter profiles, threads, and conversations for: (1) Identifying what small agency founders and eCommerce brands are discussing; (2) Understanding pain points around WordPress performance, Shopify CRO, and development bottlenecks; (3) Extracting high-performing content angles; (4) Turning insights into authority-building posts; (5) Converting Twitter intelligence into business leverage for clear content angles, strong positioning, and qualified inbound leads.

tools.name: apply_patch
tools.description: Apply a patch to one or more files using the apply_patch format. The input should include *** Begin Patch and *** End Patch markers.

input.description: Patch content using the *** Begin Patch/End Patch format.

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

tools.name: memory_get
tools.description: Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.

tools.name: memory_search
tools.description: Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. Optional `corpus=wiki` or `corpus=all` also searches registered compiled-wiki supplements. `corpus=memory` restricts hits to indexed memory files (excludes session transcript chunks from ranking). `corpus=sessions` restricts hits to indexed session transcripts (same visibility rules as session history tools). If response has disabled=true, memory retrieval is unavailable and should be surfaced to the user.

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

tools.name: session_status
tools.description: Show /status-like card for current/visible session: model, usage, time, cost, tasks. Use `sessionKey="current"` for current session; UI labels like `openclaw-tui` are not keys. `model` sets session override; `model=default` resets. Use for active model/session config questions.

tools.name: sessions_list
tools.description: List visible sessions; filter by kind, label, agentId, search, activity. Use before sessions_history or sessions_send target selection.

tools.name: sessions_send
tools.description: Send message to visible session by sessionKey/label, or configured agent by agentId; sessionKey wins when redundant label metadata is present. Thread-scoped chats rejected; target parent channel session. Creates missing configured-agent main session; waits for reply when available.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch URL and extract readable markdown/text. Lightweight page access; no browser automation.

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search web for current info; returns normalized provider results.

tools.name: write
tools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

content.text:
Ken! 👋

Yeah, foodie agent — I can help with restaurant recs, menus, cuisines, that sort of thing. What are we thinking?

tools.name: apply_patch
tools.description: Apply a patch to one or more files using the apply_patch format. The input should include *** Begin Patch and *** End Patch markers.

input.description: Patch content using the *** Begin Patch/End Patch format.

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

tools.name: memory_get
tools.description: Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.

tools.name: memory_search
tools.description: Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. Optional `corpus=wiki` or `corpus=all` also searches registered compiled-wiki supplements. `corpus=memory` restricts hits to indexed memory files (excludes session transcript chunks from ranking). `corpus=sessions` restricts hits to indexed session transcripts (same visibility rules as session history tools). If response has disabled=true, memory retrieval is unavailable and should be surfaced to the user.

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

tools.name: session_status
tools.description: Show /status-like card for current/visible session: model, usage, time, cost, tasks. Use `sessionKey="current"` for current session; UI labels like `openclaw-tui` are not keys. `model` sets session override; `model=default` resets. Use for active model/session config questions.

tools.name: sessions_list
tools.description: List visible sessions; filter by kind, label, agentId, search, activity. Use before sessions_history or sessions_send target selection.

tools.name: sessions_send
tools.description: Send message to visible session by sessionKey/label, or configured agent by agentId; sessionKey wins when redundant label metadata is present. Thread-scoped chats rejected; target parent channel session. Creates missing configured-agent main session; waits for reply when available.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch URL and extract readable markdown/text. Lightweight page access; no browser automation.

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search web for current info; returns normalized provider results.

tools.name: write
tools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

tools.name: apply_patch
tools.description: Apply a patch to one or more files using the apply_patch format. The input should include *** Begin Patch and *** End Patch markers.

input.description: Patch content using the *** Begin Patch/End Patch format.

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

tools.name: memory_get
tools.description: Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.

tools.name: memory_search
tools.description: Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. Optional `corpus=wiki` or `corpus=all` also searches registered compiled-wiki supplements. `corpus=memory` restricts hits to indexed memory files (excludes session transcript chunks from ranking). `corpus=sessions` restricts hits to indexed session transcripts (same visibility rules as session history tools). If response has disabled=true, memory retrieval is unavailable and should be surfaced to the user.

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

tools.name: session_status
tools.description: Show /status-like card for current/visible session: model, usage, time, cost, tasks. Use `sessionKey="current"` for current session; UI labels like `openclaw-tui` are not keys. `model` sets session override; `model=default` resets. Use for active model/session config questions.

tools.name: sessions_list
tools.description: List visible sessions; filter by kind, label, agentId, search, activity. Use before sessions_history or sessions_send target selection.

tools.name: sessions_send
tools.description: Send message to visible session by sessionKey/label, or configured agent by agentId; sessionKey wins when redundant label metadata is present. Thread-scoped chats rejected; target parent channel session. Creates missing configured-agent main session; waits for reply when available.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch URL and extract readable markdown/text. Lightweight page access; no browser automation.

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search web for current info; returns normalized provider results.

tools.name: write
tools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

entries.name: Bonjour Gateway Discovery
entries.description: Advertise the local OpenClaw gateway over Bonjour/mDNS.

entries.name: Browser
entries.description: Default browser tool plugin

entries.name: Canvas
entries.description: Experimental Canvas control and A2UI rendering surfaces for paired nodes.

entries.name: Device Pair
entries.description: QR/bootstrap pairing helpers for OpenClaw devices

entries.name: File Transfer
entries.description: Fetch, list, and write files on paired nodes via dedicated node commands.

entries.name: Memory (Core)
entries.description: File-backed memory search tools and CLI

entries.name: Ollama Provider
entries.description: Bundled Ollama provider plugin

entries.name: Phone Control
entries.description: Temporary allowlist control for phone automation commands

entries.name: Talk Voice
entries.description: Command helpers for managing Talk voice configuration

entries.name: Telegram
entries.description: Telegram channel plugin

entries.name: browser-automation
entries.description: Use when controlling web pages with the OpenClaw browser tool, especially multi-step flows, login checks, tab management, or recovery from stale refs/timeouts.

entries.name: canvas
entries.description: Present HTML on connected OpenClaw node canvases, navigate/eval/snapshot, and debug canvas host URLs.

entries.name: clawhub
entries.description: Search ClawHub for skills when a requested capability is not already available; install, verify, update, publish, or sync skills.

entries.name: diagram-maker
entries.description: Create SVG/HTML or Excalidraw diagrams for concepts, architecture, flows, and whiteboards.

entries.name: healthcheck
entries.description: Audit/harden OpenClaw hosts: SSH, firewall, updates, exposure, backups, disk encryption, gateway security.

entries.name: meme-maker
entries.description: Search meme templates, suggest formats, and generate local or hosted image memes.

entries.name: node-connect
entries.description: Diagnose OpenClaw Android, iOS, or macOS node pairing, QR/setup code, route, auth, and connection failures.

entries.name: node-inspect-debugger
entries.description: Debug Node.js with node inspect, --inspect, breakpoints, CDP, heap, and CPU profiles.

entries.name: notion
entries.description: Notion API for creating and managing pages, databases, and blocks.

entries.name: office-docs
entries.description: Generate and manipulate office documents (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX) for professional reports, presentations, and data exports. Use when: (1) Creating PDFs from text/images, (2) Generating Word documents, (3) Creating Excel spreadsheets with formulas, (4) Building PowerPoint presentations, (5) Extracting content from office documents.

entries.name: openclaw-slides
entries.description: Create stunning, animation-rich HTML presentations from scratch or convert PowerPoint files (.ppt/.pptx) to beautiful web slides. Use when the user wants to build a pitch deck, presentation, slideshow, or slide deck — or convert an existing PPT to a web presentation. Generates zero-dependency single HTML files with keyboard/touch navigation and scroll-triggered animations. Style options include Neon Cyber, Bold Signal, Swiss Modern, Paper & Ink, and 8 more curated presets.

entries.name: oracle
entries.description: Use the @steipete/oracle CLI to bundle a prompt plus the right files and get a second-model review (API or browser) for debugging, refactors, design checks, or cross-validation.

entries.name: PDF Generator
entries.description: Generate professional PDFs from Markdown, HTML, data, or code. Reports, invoices, contracts, and documents with best practices.

entries.name: python-debugpy
entries.description: Debug Python with pdb, breakpoint(), post-mortem inspection, and debugpy remote attach.

entries.name: skill-creator
entries.description: Create, edit, audit, tidy, validate, or restructure AgentSkills and SKILL.md files.

entries.name: spike
entries.description: Run throwaway prototypes to validate feasibility, compare approaches, and report a verdict.

entries.name: taskflow
entries.description: Coordinate multi-step detached tasks as one durable TaskFlow job with owner context, state, waits, and child tasks.

entries.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
entries.description: Example TaskFlow pattern for inbox triage, intent routing, waiting on replies, and later summaries.

entries.name: weather
entries.description: Current weather and forecasts with web_fetch, falling back to wttr.in curl for locations, rain, temperature, travel planning.

entries.name: xurl
entries.description: A Twitter research and content intelligence skill focused on attracting WordPress and Shopify clients. Use to analyze Twitter profiles, threads, and conversations for: (1) Identifying what small agency founders and eCommerce brands are discussing; (2) Understanding pain points around WordPress performance, Shopify CRO, and development bottlenecks; (3) Extracting high-performing content angles; (4) Turning insights into authority-building posts; (5) Converting Twitter intelligence into business leverage for clear content angles, strong positioning, and qualified inbound leads.

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

tools.name: session_status
tools.description: Show /status-like card for current/visible session: model, usage, time, cost, tasks. Use `sessionKey="current"` for current session; UI labels like `openclaw-tui` are not keys. `model` sets session override; `model=default` resets. Use for active model/session config questions.

tools.name: sessions_list
tools.description: List visible sessions; filter by kind, label, agentId, search, activity. Use before sessions_history or sessions_send target selection.

tools.name: sessions_send
tools.description: Send message to visible session by sessionKey/label, or configured agent by agentId; sessionKey wins when redundant label metadata is present. Thread-scoped chats rejected; target parent channel session. Creates missing configured-agent main session; waits for reply when available.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch URL and extract readable markdown/text. Lightweight page access; no browser automation.

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search web for current info; returns normalized provider results.

tools.name: write
tools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

Hey. What about the crew name and the below questions that I parked earlier - for the group?

Now, the fun part. Tell me about you and the crew:

• How many of you? Just a regular dinner duo, or is it a squad?

Cuisine leanings? Any no-gos? (Seafood only? Love a good steak? Open to anything?)

• Vibe preference? Hidden laneway gems, white-tablecloth, noisy wine bars, bistros with paper tablecloths?

Budget range? Casual Tuesday or blowout Saturday?

Any recent wins/losses? Any Melbourne restaurants you've loved or hated lately - gives me a compass

messages.content: Foodie?

content.text:
Ken! 👋

Yeah, foodie agent — I can help with restaurant recs, menus, cuisines, that sort of thing. What are we thinking?

messages.content: Perfect. Our group is now setup. You have Nick in the group as well

content.text:
Hey Nick! 👋 Welcome to the Dinner Crew 🍽️

You two are the founding members so far. What kind of food are we into? I can start scoping out options whenever we're ready to plan a feed.

messages.content: You have a kickoff - discovery task for us first isn't it?

content.text:
You know me well. Quick discovery round for the Dinner Crew:

**1. Cuisine vibe?** — Any preferences or dealbreakers? (Asian, Italian, Modern Aussie, something specific?)

**2. Location?** — Melbourne CBD? Suburbs? Open to travel?

**3. Budget?** — Casual eats, mid-range, or splurge territory?

**4. Occasion?** — Just a regular catch-up, or celebrating something?

Nick, feel free to chime in with your thoughts too!

assistantTexts:
Good catch — couple of things to run by the group:

**1. Crew name** — Ken floated "Dinner Crew" as the group name, but it was a placeholder. Other ideas? "Melb Supper Club", "The Forking Crew", "Feed Me Club", something else? Or stick with Dinner Crew?

**2. The questions Ken parked earlier:**

- **Headcount** — just us two so far, or are there more coming?
- **Cuisine** — any hard no's? Ken, any preferences on your end, Nick?
- **Vibe** — hidden laneway, white tablecloth, noisy wine bar, casual bistro?
- **Budget** — casual Tuesday or blowout Saturday territory?
- **Recent hits/misses** — any Melbourne restaurants you've loved or hated lately to give me a compass?

Nick, keen to hear your thoughts when you're around!

entries.description: Advertise the local OpenClaw gateway over Bonjour/mDNS.

entries.name: Browser
entries.description: Default browser tool plugin

entries.name: Canvas
entries.description: Experimental Canvas control and A2UI rendering surfaces for paired nodes.

entries.name: Device Pair
entries.description: QR/bootstrap pairing helpers for OpenClaw devices

entries.name: File Transfer
entries.description: Fetch, list, and write files on paired nodes via dedicated node commands.

entries.name: Memory (Core)
entries.description: File-backed memory search tools and CLI

entries.name: Ollama Provider
entries.description: Bundled Ollama provider plugin

entries.name: Phone Control
entries.description: Temporary allowlist control for phone automation commands

entries.name: Talk Voice
entries.description: Command helpers for managing Talk voice configuration

entries.name: Telegram
entries.description: Telegram channel plugin

entries.name: browser-automation
entries.description: Use when controlling web pages with the OpenClaw browser tool, especially multi-step flows, login checks, tab management, or recovery from stale refs/timeouts.

entries.name: canvas
entries.description: Present HTML on connected OpenClaw node canvases, navigate/eval/snapshot, and debug canvas host URLs.

entries.name: clawhub
entries.description: Search ClawHub for skills when a requested capability is not already available; install, verify, update, publish, or sync skills.

entries.name: diagram-maker
entries.description: Create SVG/HTML or Excalidraw diagrams for concepts, architecture, flows, and whiteboards.

entries.name: healthcheck
entries.description: Audit/harden OpenClaw hosts: SSH, firewall, updates, exposure, backups, disk encryption, gateway security.

entries.name: meme-maker
entries.description: Search meme templates, suggest formats, and generate local or hosted image memes.

entries.name: node-connect
entries.description: Diagnose OpenClaw Android, iOS, or macOS node pairing, QR/setup code, route, auth, and connection failures.

entries.name: node-inspect-debugger
entries.description: Debug Node.js with node inspect, --inspect, breakpoints, CDP, heap, and CPU profiles.

entries.name: notion
entries.description: Notion API for creating and managing pages, databases, and blocks.

entries.name: office-docs
entries.description: Generate and manipulate office documents (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX) for professional reports, presentations, and data exports. Use when: (1) Creating PDFs from text/images, (2) Generating Word documents, (3) Creating Excel spreadsheets with formulas, (4) Building PowerPoint presentations, (5) Extracting content from office documents.

entries.name: openclaw-slides
entries.description: Create stunning, animation-rich HTML presentations from scratch or convert PowerPoint files (.ppt/.pptx) to beautiful web slides. Use when the user wants to build a pitch deck, presentation, slideshow, or slide deck — or convert an existing PPT to a web presentation. Generates zero-dependency single HTML files with keyboard/touch navigation and scroll-triggered animations. Style options include Neon Cyber, Bold Signal, Swiss Modern, Paper & Ink, and 8 more curated presets.

entries.name: oracle
entries.description: Use the @steipete/oracle CLI to bundle a prompt plus the right files and get a second-model review (API or browser) for debugging, refactors, design checks, or cross-validation.

entries.name: PDF Generator
entries.description: Generate professional PDFs from Markdown, HTML, data, or code. Reports, invoices, contracts, and documents with best practices.

entries.name: python-debugpy
entries.description: Debug Python with pdb, breakpoint(), post-mortem inspection, and debugpy remote attach.

entries.name: skill-creator
entries.description: Create, edit, audit, tidy, validate, or restructure AgentSkills and SKILL.md files.

entries.name: spike
entries.description: Run throwaway prototypes to validate feasibility, compare approaches, and report a verdict.

entries.name: taskflow
entries.description: Coordinate multi-step detached tasks as one durable TaskFlow job with owner context, state, waits, and child tasks.

entries.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
entries.description: Example TaskFlow pattern for inbox triage, intent routing, waiting on replies, and later summaries.

entries.name: weather
entries.description: Current weather and forecasts with web_fetch, falling back to wttr.in curl for locations, rain, temperature, travel planning.

entries.name: xurl
entries.description: A Twitter research and content intelligence skill focused on attracting WordPress and Shopify clients. Use to analyze Twitter profiles, threads, and conversations for: (1) Identifying what small agency founders and eCommerce brands are discussing; (2) Understanding pain points around WordPress performance, Shopify CRO, and development bottlenecks; (3) Extracting high-performing content angles; (4) Turning insights into authority-building posts; (5) Converting Twitter intelligence into business leverage for clear content angles, strong positioning, and qualified inbound leads.

tools.name: apply_patch
tools.description: Apply a patch to one or more files using the apply_patch format. The input should include *** Begin Patch and *** End Patch markers.

input.description: Patch content using the *** Begin Patch/End Patch format.

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

tools.name: memory_get
tools.description: Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.

tools.name: memory_search
tools.description: Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. Optional `corpus=wiki` or `corpus=all` also searches registered compiled-wiki supplements. `corpus=memory` restricts hits to indexed memory files (excludes session transcript chunks from ranking). `corpus=sessions` restricts hits to indexed session transcripts (same visibility rules as session history tools). If response has disabled=true, memory retrieval is unavailable and should be surfaced to the user.

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

tools.name: session_status
tools.description: Show /status-like card for current/visible session: model, usage, time, cost, tasks. Use `sessionKey="current"` for current session; UI labels like `openclaw-tui` are not keys. `model` sets session override; `model=default` resets. Use for active model/session config questions.

tools.name: sessions_list
tools.description: List visible sessions; filter by kind, label, agentId, search, activity. Use before sessions_history or sessions_send target selection.

tools.name: sessions_send
tools.description: Send message to visible session by sessionKey/label, or configured agent by agentId; sessionKey wins when redundant label metadata is present. Thread-scoped chats rejected; target parent channel session. Creates missing configured-agent main session; waits for reply when available.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch URL and extract readable markdown/text. Lightweight page access; no browser automation.

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search web for current info; returns normalized provider results.

tools.name: write
tools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

messages.content: Foodie?

content.text:
Ken! 👋

Yeah, foodie agent — I can help with restaurant recs, menus, cuisines, that sort of thing. What are we thinking?

messages.content: Perfect. Our group is now setup. You have Nick in the group as well

content.text:
Hey Nick! 👋 Welcome to the Dinner Crew 🍽️

You two are the founding members so far. What kind of food are we into? I can start scoping out options whenever we're ready to plan a feed.

messages.content: You have a kickoff - discovery task for us first isn't it?

content.text:
You know me well. Quick discovery round for the Dinner Crew:

**1. Cuisine vibe?** — Any preferences or dealbreakers? (Asian, Italian, Modern Aussie, something specific?)

**2. Location?** — Melbourne CBD? Suburbs? Open to travel?

**3. Budget?** — Casual eats, mid-range, or splurge territory?

**4. Occasion?** — Just a regular catch-up, or celebrating something?

Nick, feel free to chime in with your thoughts too!

messages.content:
Hey. What about the crew name and the below questions that I parked earlier - for the group?

Now, the fun part. Tell me about you and the crew:

• How many of you? Just a regular dinner duo, or is it a squad?

Cuisine leanings? Any no-gos? (Seafood only? Love a good steak? Open to anything?)

• Vibe preference? Hidden laneway gems, white-tablecloth, noisy wine bars, bistros with paper tablecloths?

Budget range? Casual Tuesday or blowout Saturday?

Any recent wins/losses? Any Melbourne restaurants you've loved or hated lately - gives me a compass

content.text:
Good catch — couple of things to run by the group:

**1. Crew name** — Ken floated "Dinner Crew" as the group name, but it was a placeholder. Other ideas? "Melb Supper Club", "The Forking Crew", "Feed Me Club", something else? Or stick with Dinner Crew?

**2. The questions Ken parked earlier:**

- **Headcount** — just us two so far, or are there more coming?
- **Cuisine** — any hard no's? Ken, any preferences on your end, Nick?
- **Vibe** — hidden laneway, white tablecloth, noisy wine bar, casual bistro?
- **Budget** — casual Tuesday or blowout Saturday territory?
- **Recent hits/misses** — any Melbourne restaurants you've loved or hated lately to give me a compass?

Nick, keen to hear your thoughts when you're around!

assistantTexts:
Right, Damo's a holdout classic 😅

Couple of ways to handle it:

1. **Bridge him in** — I can't do SMS bridging directly, but you could forward/SS him screenshots of the convo when decisions need his input. Low-fi but works.

2. **He just needs to be in the loop for decisions, not every meme** — we could pick restaurants as a group here, then you relay the final shortlist to him via SMS/WhatsApp for a thumbs up before booking.

3. **The nuclear option** — tell him the crew name is "Damo's Not On Telegram Club" until he joins 😂

What's his objection? Privacy thing, another app fatigue, or just being difficult?

entries.description: QR/bootstrap pairing helpers for OpenClaw devices

entries.description: Fetch, list, and write files on paired nodes via dedicated node commands.

entries.description: File-backed memory search tools and CLI

entries.description: Bundled Ollama provider plugin

entries.description: Temporary allowlist control for phone automation commands

entries.description: Command helpers for managing Talk voice configuration

entries.name: Telegram
entries.description: Telegram channel plugin

entries.name: browser-automation
entries.description: Use when controlling web pages with the OpenClaw browser tool, especially multi-step flows, login checks, tab management, or recovery from stale refs/timeouts.

entries.name: canvas
entries.description: Present HTML on connected OpenClaw node canvases, navigate/eval/snapshot, and debug canvas host URLs.

entries.name: clawhub
entries.description: Search ClawHub for skills when a requested capability is not already available; install, verify, update, publish, or sync skills.

entries.name: diagram-maker
entries.description: Create SVG/HTML or Excalidraw diagrams for concepts, architecture, flows, and whiteboards.

entries.name: healthcheck
entries.description: Audit/harden OpenClaw hosts: SSH, firewall, updates, exposure, backups, disk encryption, gateway security.

entries.name: meme-maker
entries.description: Search meme templates, suggest formats, and generate local or hosted image memes.

entries.name: node-connect
entries.description: Diagnose OpenClaw Android, iOS, or macOS node pairing, QR/setup code, route, auth, and connection failures.

entries.name: node-inspect-debugger
entries.description: Debug Node.js with node inspect, --inspect, breakpoints, CDP, heap, and CPU profiles.

entries.name: notion
entries.description: Notion API for creating and managing pages, databases, and blocks.

entries.name: office-docs
entries.description: Generate and manipulate office documents (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX) for professional reports, presentations, and data exports. Use when: (1) Creating PDFs from text/images, (2) Generating Word documents, (3) Creating Excel spreadsheets with formulas, (4) Building PowerPoint presentations, (5) Extracting content from office documents.

entries.name: openclaw-slides
entries.description: Create stunning, animation-rich HTML presentations from scratch or convert PowerPoint files (.ppt/.pptx) to beautiful web slides. Use when the user wants to build a pitch deck, presentation, slideshow, or slide deck — or convert an existing PPT to a web presentation. Generates zero-dependency single HTML files with keyboard/touch navigation and scroll-triggered animations. Style options include Neon Cyber, Bold Signal, Swiss Modern, Paper & Ink, and 8 more curated presets.

entries.name: oracle
entries.description: Use the @steipete/oracle CLI to bundle a prompt plus the right files and get a second-model review (API or browser) for debugging, refactors, design checks, or cross-validation.

entries.name: PDF Generator
entries.description: Generate professional PDFs from Markdown, HTML, data, or code. Reports, invoices, contracts, and documents with best practices.

entries.name: python-debugpy
entries.description: Debug Python with pdb, breakpoint(), post-mortem inspection, and debugpy remote attach.

entries.name: skill-creator
entries.description: Create, edit, audit, tidy, validate, or restructure AgentSkills and SKILL.md files.

entries.name: spike
entries.description: Run throwaway prototypes to validate feasibility, compare approaches, and report a verdict.

entries.name: taskflow
entries.description: Coordinate multi-step detached tasks as one durable TaskFlow job with owner context, state, waits, and child tasks.

entries.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
entries.description: Example TaskFlow pattern for inbox triage, intent routing, waiting on replies, and later summaries.

entries.name: weather
entries.description: Current weather and forecasts with web_fetch, falling back to wttr.in curl for locations, rain, temperature, travel planning.

entries.name: xurl
entries.description: A Twitter research and content intelligence skill focused on attracting WordPress and Shopify clients. Use to analyze Twitter profiles, threads, and conversations for: (1) Identifying what small agency founders and eCommerce brands are discussing; (2) Understanding pain points around WordPress performance, Shopify CRO, and development bottlenecks; (3) Extracting high-performing content angles; (4) Turning insights into authority-building posts; (5) Converting Twitter intelligence into business leverage for clear content angles, strong positioning, and qualified inbound leads.

tools.name: apply_patch
tools.description: Apply a patch to one or more files using the apply_patch format. The input should include *** Begin Patch and *** End Patch markers.

input.description: Patch content using the *** Begin Patch/End Patch format.

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

tools.name: memory_get
tools.description: Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.

tools.name: memory_search
tools.description: Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. Optional `corpus=wiki` or `corpus=all` also searches registered compiled-wiki supplements. `corpus=memory` restricts hits to indexed memory files (excludes session transcript chunks from ranking). `corpus=sessions` restricts hits to indexed session transcripts (same visibility rules as session history tools). If response has disabled=true, memory retrieval is unavailable and should be surfaced to the user.

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

tools.name: session_status
tools.description: Show /status-like card for current/visible session: model, usage, time, cost, tasks. Use `sessionKey="current"` for current session; UI labels like `openclaw-tui` are not keys. `model` sets session override; `model=default` resets. Use for active model/session config questions.

tools.name: sessions_list
tools.description: List visible sessions; filter by kind, label, agentId, search, activity. Use before sessions_history or sessions_send target selection.

tools.name: sessions_send
tools.description: Send message to visible session by sessionKey/label, or configured agent by agentId; sessionKey wins when redundant label metadata is present. Thread-scoped chats rejected; target parent channel session. Creates missing configured-agent main session; waits for reply when available.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch URL and extract readable markdown/text. Lightweight page access; no browser automation.

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search web for current info; returns normalized provider results.

tools.name: write
tools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

assistantTexts:
Haha the principled stand against yet another messaging app. Classic Damo.

I'd say **option 2** is the pragmatic play — we do the research and shortlist here, then you toss him the final 2-3 choices over SMS for a quick 👍 before locking in a booking. He's not in the chatter, but he's in the decisions.

When we get to booking time I can also check availability/phone numbers for whatever spot we land on so the SMS to Damo has all the details ready to go.

Now — while we wait for Damo to come around (any year now)... you two wanna start answering those discovery questions so I can start hunting?

entries.name: Bonjour Gateway Discovery
entries.description: Advertise the local OpenClaw gateway over Bonjour/mDNS.

entries.name: Browser
entries.description: Default browser tool plugin

entries.name: Canvas
entries.description: Experimental Canvas control and A2UI rendering surfaces for paired nodes.

entries.name: Device Pair
entries.description: QR/bootstrap pairing helpers for OpenClaw devices

entries.name: File Transfer
entries.description: Fetch, list, and write files on paired nodes via dedicated node commands.

entries.name: Memory (Core)
entries.description: File-backed memory search tools and CLI

entries.name: Ollama Provider
entries.description: Bundled Ollama provider plugin

entries.name: Phone Control
entries.description: Temporary allowlist control for phone automation commands

entries.name: Talk Voice
entries.description: Command helpers for managing Talk voice configuration

entries.name: Telegram
entries.description: Telegram channel plugin

entries.name: browser-automation
entries.description: Use when controlling web pages with the OpenClaw browser tool, especially multi-step flows, login checks, tab management, or recovery from stale refs/timeouts.

entries.name: canvas
entries.description: Present HTML on connected OpenClaw node canvases, navigate/eval/snapshot, and debug canvas host URLs.

entries.name: clawhub
entries.description: Search ClawHub for skills when a requested capability is not already available; install, verify, update, publish, or sync skills.

entries.name: diagram-maker
entries.description: Create SVG/HTML or Excalidraw diagrams for concepts, architecture, flows, and whiteboards.

entries.name: healthcheck
entries.description: Audit/harden OpenClaw hosts: SSH, firewall, updates, exposure, backups, disk encryption, gateway security.

entries.name: meme-maker
entries.description: Search meme templates, suggest formats, and generate local or hosted image memes.

entries.name: node-connect
entries.description: Diagnose OpenClaw Android, iOS, or macOS node pairing, QR/setup code, route, auth, and connection failures.

entries.name: node-inspect-debugger
entries.description: Debug Node.js with node inspect, --inspect, breakpoints, CDP, heap, and CPU profiles.

entries.name: notion
entries.description: Notion API for creating and managing pages, databases, and blocks.

entries.name: office-docs
entries.description: Generate and manipulate office documents (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX) for professional reports, presentations, and data exports. Use when: (1) Creating PDFs from text/images, (2) Generating Word documents, (3) Creating Excel spreadsheets with formulas, (4) Building PowerPoint presentations, (5) Extracting content from office documents.

entries.name: openclaw-slides
entries.description: Create stunning, animation-rich HTML presentations from scratch or convert PowerPoint files (.ppt/.pptx) to beautiful web slides. Use when the user wants to build a pitch deck, presentation, slideshow, or slide deck — or convert an existing PPT to a web presentation. Generates zero-dependency single HTML files with keyboard/touch navigation and scroll-triggered animations. Style options include Neon Cyber, Bold Signal, Swiss Modern, Paper & Ink, and 8 more curated presets.

entries.name: oracle
entries.description: Use the @steipete/oracle CLI to bundle a prompt plus the right files and get a second-model review (API or browser) for debugging, refactors, design checks, or cross-validation.

entries.name: PDF Generator
entries.description: Generate professional PDFs from Markdown, HTML, data, or code. Reports, invoices, contracts, and documents with best practices.

entries.name: python-debugpy
entries.description: Debug Python with pdb, breakpoint(), post-mortem inspection, and debugpy remote attach.

entries.name: skill-creator
entries.description: Create, edit, audit, tidy, validate, or restructure AgentSkills and SKILL.md files.

entries.name: spike
entries.description: Run throwaway prototypes to validate feasibility, compare approaches, and report a verdict.

entries.name: taskflow
entries.description: Coordinate multi-step detached tasks as one durable TaskFlow job with owner context, state, waits, and child tasks.

entries.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
entries.description: Example TaskFlow pattern for inbox triage, intent routing, waiting on replies, and later summaries.

entries.name: weather
entries.description: Current weather and forecasts with web_fetch, falling back to wttr.in curl for locations, rain, temperature, travel planning.

entries.name: xurl
entries.description: A Twitter research and content intelligence skill focused on attracting WordPress and Shopify clients. Use to analyze Twitter profiles, threads, and conversations for: (1) Identifying what small agency founders and eCommerce brands are discussing; (2) Understanding pain points around WordPress performance, Shopify CRO, and development bottlenecks; (3) Extracting high-performing content angles; (4) Turning insights into authority-building posts; (5) Converting Twitter intelligence into business leverage for clear content angles, strong positioning, and qualified inbound leads.

messages.content: Foodie?

content.text:
Ken! 👋

Yeah, foodie agent — I can help with restaurant recs, menus, cuisines, that sort of thing. What are we thinking?

messages.content: Perfect. Our group is now setup. You have Nick in the group as well

content.text:
Hey Nick! 👋 Welcome to the Dinner Crew 🍽️

You two are the founding members so far. What kind of food are we into? I can start scoping out options whenever we're ready to plan a feed.

messages.content: You have a kickoff - discovery task for us first isn't it?

content.text:
You know me well. Quick discovery round for the Dinner Crew:

**1. Cuisine vibe?** — Any preferences or dealbreakers? (Asian, Italian, Modern Aussie, something specific?)

**2. Location?** — Melbourne CBD? Suburbs? Open to travel?

**3. Budget?** — Casual eats, mid-range, or splurge territory?

**4. Occasion?** — Just a regular catch-up, or celebrating something?

Nick, feel free to chime in with your thoughts too!

messages.content:
Hey. What about the crew name and the below questions that I parked earlier - for the group?

Now, the fun part. Tell me about you and the crew:

• How many of you? Just a regular dinner duo, or is it a squad?

Cuisine leanings? Any no-gos? (Seafood only? Love a good steak? Open to anything?)

• Vibe preference? Hidden laneway gems, white-tablecloth, noisy wine bars, bistros with paper tablecloths?

Budget range? Casual Tuesday or blowout Saturday?

Any recent wins/losses? Any Melbourne restaurants you've loved or hated lately - gives me a compass

content.text:
Good catch — couple of things to run by the group:

**1. Crew name** — Ken floated "Dinner Crew" as the group name, but it was a placeholder. Other ideas? "Melb Supper Club", "The Forking Crew", "Feed Me Club", something else? Or stick with Dinner Crew?

**2. The questions Ken parked earlier:**

- **Headcount** — just us two so far, or are there more coming?
- **Cuisine** — any hard no's? Ken, any preferences on your end, Nick?
- **Vibe** — hidden laneway, white tablecloth, noisy wine bar, casual bistro?
- **Budget** — casual Tuesday or blowout Saturday territory?
- **Recent hits/misses** — any Melbourne restaurants you've loved or hated lately to give me a compass?

Nick, keen to hear your thoughts when you're around!

messages.content: First off, crew is still incomplete. We're still missing Damo, he refuses to install and use telegram. That's a blocker I'm still trying to figure out.

content.text:
Right, Damo's a holdout classic 😅

Couple of ways to handle it:

1. **Bridge him in** — I can't do SMS bridging directly, but you could forward/SS him screenshots of the convo when decisions need his input. Low-fi but works.

2. **He just needs to be in the loop for decisions, not every meme** — we could pick restaurants as a group here, then you relay the final shortlist to him via SMS/WhatsApp for a thumbs up before booking.

3. **The nuclear option** — tell him the crew name is "Damo's Not On Telegram Club" until he joins 😂

What's his objection? Privacy thing, another app fatigue, or just being difficult?

messages.content: App fatigue and his taking a stand. Hahaha

content.text:
Haha the principled stand against yet another messaging app. Classic Damo.

I'd say **option 2** is the pragmatic play — we do the research and shortlist here, then you toss him the final 2-3 choices over SMS for a quick 👍 before locking in a booking. He's not in the chatter, but he's in the decisions.

When we get to booking time I can also check availability/phone numbers for whatever spot we land on so the SMS to Damo has all the details ready to go.

Now — while we wait for Damo to come around (any year now)... you two wanna start answering those discovery questions so I can start hunting?

tools.name: apply_patch
tools.description: Apply a patch to one or more files using the apply_patch format. The input should include *** Begin Patch and *** End Patch markers.

input.description: Patch content using the *** Begin Patch/End Patch format.

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

tools.name: memory_get
tools.description: Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.

tools.name: memory_search
tools.description: Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. Optional `corpus=wiki` or `corpus=all` also searches registered compiled-wiki supplements. `corpus=memory` restricts hits to indexed memory files (excludes session transcript chunks from ranking). `corpus=sessions` restricts hits to indexed session transcripts (same visibility rules as session history tools). If response has disabled=true, memory retrieval is unavailable and should be surfaced to the user.

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

tools.name: session_status
tools.description: Show /status-like card for current/visible session: model, usage, time, cost, tasks. Use `sessionKey="current"` for current session; UI labels like `openclaw-tui` are not keys. `model` sets session override; `model=default` resets. Use for active model/session config questions.

tools.name: sessions_list
tools.description: List visible sessions; filter by kind, label, agentId, search, activity. Use before sessions_history or sessions_send target selection.

tools.name: sessions_send
tools.description: Send message to visible session by sessionKey/label, or configured agent by agentId; sessionKey wins when redundant label metadata is present. Thread-scoped chats rejected; target parent channel session. Creates missing configured-agent main session; waits for reply when available.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch URL and extract readable markdown/text. Lightweight page access; no browser automation.

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search web for current info; returns normalized provider results.

tools.name: write
tools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

assistantTexts:
Nice, so the tradition is already rolling — once a month, the three musketeers. Squires Albert Park three times in a row though 😂 time to break the cycle?

Let me get the ball rolling. I'll look up Squires for context on what you guys like, then scout some alternatives in a similar vein but different enough to keep it interesting.

Right, search is on the fritz — but I know Squires well enough. It's a solid steakhouse / modern Australian in the old Albert Park hotel. Classic pub-gastropub with quality steaks and a good wine list. So you guys clearly like **steak + good atmosphere + easy catch-up vibes**.

To narrow things down before I start digging:

- **Same steakhouse energy but somewhere new?** (other top notch steakhouses around town)
- **Or completely different cuisine to mix it up?** (Italian, Asian, something else?)
- **South-side preference?** (Albert Park, St Kilda, South Melbourne) or happy to go CBD/north?
- **Date-wise** — roughly when's the next one due? End of July?

Style options include Neon Cyber, Bold Signal, Swiss Modern, Paper & Ink, and 8 more curated presets.

prompting.skillsPrompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
If a skill's <version> differs from a previous turn, re-read its SKILL.md before using it.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>

    <name>browser-automation</name>
    <description>Use when controlling web pages with the OpenClaw browser tool, especially multi-step flows, login checks, tab management, or recovery from stale refs/timeouts.</description>
    <location>~/.openclaw/plugin-skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md</location>
    <version>sha256:608bed60aca52631</version>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>canvas</name>

    <version>sha256:7419e23f242397b3</version>
  </skill>

  </skill>

I'll leave you in the hands of Nick next. Let him pitch in and you work with him a bit more

messages.content: Foodie?

content.text:
Ken! 👋

Yeah, foodie agent — I can help with restaurant recs, menus, cuisines, that sort of thing. What are we thinking?

messages.content: Perfect. Our group is now setup. You have Nick in the group as well

content.text:
Hey Nick! 👋 Welcome to the Dinner Crew 🍽️

You two are the founding members so far. What kind of food are we into? I can start scoping out options whenever we're ready to plan a feed.

messages.content: You have a kickoff - discovery task for us first isn't it?

content.text:
You know me well. Quick discovery round for the Dinner Crew:

**1. Cuisine vibe?** — Any preferences or dealbreakers? (Asian, Italian, Modern Aussie, something specific?)

**2. Location?** — Melbourne CBD? Suburbs? Open to travel?

**3. Budget?** — Casual eats, mid-range, or splurge territory?

**4. Occasion?** — Just a regular catch-up, or celebrating something?

Nick, feel free to chime in with your thoughts too!

messages.content:
Hey. What about the crew name and the below questions that I parked earlier - for the group?

Now, the fun part. Tell me about you and the crew:

• How many of you? Just a regular dinner duo, or is it a squad?

Cuisine leanings? Any no-gos? (Seafood only? Love a good steak? Open to anything?)

• Vibe preference? Hidden laneway gems, white-tablecloth, noisy wine bars, bistros with paper tablecloths?

Budget range? Casual Tuesday or blowout Saturday?

Any recent wins/losses? Any Melbourne restaurants you've loved or hated lately - gives me a compass

content.text:
Good catch — couple of things to run by the group:

**1. Crew name** — Ken floated "Dinner Crew" as the group name, but it was a placeholder. Other ideas? "Melb Supper Club", "The Forking Crew", "Feed Me Club", something else? Or stick with Dinner Crew?

**2. The questions Ken parked earlier:**

- **Headcount** — just us two so far, or are there more coming?
- **Cuisine** — any hard no's? Ken, any preferences on your end, Nick?
- **Vibe** — hidden laneway, white tablecloth, noisy wine bar, casual bistro?
- **Budget** — casual Tuesday or blowout Saturday territory?
- **Recent hits/misses** — any Melbourne restaurants you've loved or hated lately to give me a compass?

Nick, keen to hear your thoughts when you're around!

messages.content: First off, crew is still incomplete. We're still missing Damo, he refuses to install and use telegram. That's a blocker I'm still trying to figure out.

content.text:
Right, Damo's a holdout classic 😅

Couple of ways to handle it:

1. **Bridge him in** — I can't do SMS bridging directly, but you could forward/SS him screenshots of the convo when decisions need his input. Low-fi but works.

2. **He just needs to be in the loop for decisions, not every meme** — we could pick restaurants as a group here, then you relay the final shortlist to him via SMS/WhatsApp for a thumbs up before booking.

3. **The nuclear option** — tell him the crew name is "Damo's Not On Telegram Club" until he joins 😂

What's his objection? Privacy thing, another app fatigue, or just being difficult?

messages.content: App fatigue and his taking a stand. Hahaha

content.text:
Haha the principled stand against yet another messaging app. Classic Damo.

I'd say **option 2** is the pragmatic play — we do the research and shortlist here, then you toss him the final 2-3 choices over SMS for a quick 👍 before locking in a booking. He's not in the chatter, but he's in the decisions.

When we get to booking time I can also check availability/phone numbers for whatever spot we land on so the SMS to Damo has all the details ready to go.

Now — while we wait for Damo to come around (any year now)... you two wanna start answering those discovery questions so I can start hunting?

messages.content: We'll the trio of us have had 3 dinner sessions in the past 3 months. Once a month we try to arrange and catch up. All 3 times have been at Squires Albert Park

content.text:
Nice, so the tradition is already rolling — once a month, the three musketeers. Squires Albert Park three times in a row though 😂 time to break the cycle?

Let me get the ball rolling. I'll look up Squires for context on what you guys like, then scout some alternatives in a similar vein but different enough to keep it interesting.

arguments.query: Squires Albert Park Melbourne restaurant review
Squires Albert Park Melbourne restaurant steakhouse

content.text:
Right, search is on the fritz — but I know Squires well enough. It's a solid steakhouse / modern Australian in the old Albert Park hotel. Classic pub-gastropub with quality steaks and a good wine list. So you guys clearly like **steak + good atmosphere + easy catch-up vibes**.

To narrow things down before I start digging:

- **Same steakhouse energy but somewhere new?** (other top notch steakhouses around town)
- **Or completely different cuisine to mix it up?** (Italian, Asian, something else?)
- **South-side preference?** (Albert Park, St Kilda, South Melbourne) or happy to go CBD/north?
- **Date-wise** — roughly when's the next one due? End of July?

tools.name: apply_patch
tools.description: Apply a patch to one or more files using the apply_patch format. The input should include *** Begin Patch and *** End Patch markers.

input.description: Patch content using the *** Begin Patch/End Patch format.

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

tools.name: memory_get
tools.description: Safe exact excerpt read from MEMORY.md or memory/*.md. Defaults to a bounded excerpt when lines are omitted, includes truncation/continuation info when more content exists, and `corpus=wiki` reads from registered compiled-wiki supplements.

tools.name: memory_search
tools.description: Mandatory recall step: semantically search MEMORY.md + memory/*.md (and optional session transcripts) before answering questions about prior work, decisions, dates, people, preferences, or todos. Optional `corpus=wiki` or `corpus=all` also searches registered compiled-wiki supplements. `corpus=memory` restricts hits to indexed memory files (excludes session transcript chunks from ranking). `corpus=sessions` restricts hits to indexed session transcripts (same visibility rules as session history tools). If response has disabled=true, memory retrieval is unavailable and should be surfaced to the user.

tools.name: read
tools.description: Read the contents of a file. Supports text files and images (jpg, png, gif, webp). Images are sent as attachments. For text files, output is truncated to 2000 lines or 50KB (whichever is hit first). Use offset/limit for large files. When you need the full file, continue with offset until complete.

path.description: Path to the file to read (relative or absolute)

offset.description: Line number to start reading from (1-indexed)

limit.description: Maximum number of lines to read

tools.name: session_status
tools.description: Show /status-like card for current/visible session: model, usage, time, cost, tasks. Use `sessionKey="current"` for current session; UI labels like `openclaw-tui` are not keys. `model` sets session override; `model=default` resets. Use for active model/session config questions.

tools.name: sessions_list
tools.description: List visible sessions; filter by kind, label, agentId, search, activity. Use before sessions_history or sessions_send target selection.

tools.name: sessions_send
tools.description: Send message to visible session by sessionKey/label, or configured agent by agentId; sessionKey wins when redundant label metadata is present. Thread-scoped chats rejected; target parent channel session. Creates missing configured-agent main session; waits for reply when available.

tools.name: web_fetch
tools.description: Fetch URL and extract readable markdown/text. Lightweight page access; no browser automation.

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search web for current info; returns normalized provider results.

tools.name: write
tools.description: Write content to a file. Creates the file if it doesn't exist, overwrites if it does. Automatically creates parent directories.

path.description: Path to the file to write (relative or absolute)

content.description: Content to write to the file

entries.name: browser-automation
entries.description: Use when controlling web pages with the OpenClaw browser tool, especially multi-step flows, login checks, tab management, or recovery from stale refs/timeouts.

entries.name: canvas
entries.description: Present HTML on connected OpenClaw node canvases, navigate/eval/snapshot, and debug canvas host URLs.

entries.name: clawhub
entries.description: Search ClawHub for skills when a requested capability is not already available; install, verify, update, publish, or sync skills.

entries.name: diagram-maker
entries.description: Create SVG/HTML or Excalidraw diagrams for concepts, architecture, flows, and whiteboards.

entries.name: healthcheck
entries.description: Audit/harden OpenClaw hosts: SSH, firewall, updates, exposure, backups, disk encryption, gateway security.

entries.name: meme-maker
entries.description: Search meme templates, suggest formats, and generate local or hosted image memes.

entries.name: node-connect
entries.description: Diagnose OpenClaw Android, iOS, or macOS node pairing, QR/setup code, route, auth, and connection failures.

entries.name: node-inspect-debugger
entries.description: Debug Node.js with node inspect, --inspect, breakpoints, CDP, heap, and CPU profiles.

entries.name: notion
entries.description: Notion API for creating and managing pages, databases, and blocks.

entries.name: office-docs
entries.description: Generate and manipulate office documents (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX) for professional reports, presentations, and data exports. Use when: (1) Creating PDFs from text/images, (2) Generating Word documents, (3) Creating Excel spreadsheets with formulas, (4) Building PowerPoint presentations, (5) Extracting content from office documents.

entries.name: openclaw-slides
entries.description: Create stunning, animation-rich HTML presentations from scratch or convert PowerPoint files (.ppt/.pptx) to beautiful web slides. Use when the user wants to build a pitch deck, presentation, slideshow, or slide deck — or convert an existing PPT to a web presentation. Generates zero-dependency single HTML files with keyboard/touch navigation and scroll-triggered animations. Style options include Neon Cyber, Bold Signal, Swiss Modern, Paper & Ink, and 8 more curated presets.

entries.name: oracle
entries.description: Use the @steipete/oracle CLI to bundle a prompt plus the right files and get a second-model review (API or browser) for debugging, refactors, design checks, or cross-validation.

entries.name: PDF Generator
entries.description: Generate professional PDFs from Markdown, HTML, data, or code. Reports, invoices, contracts, and documents with best practices.

entries.name: python-debugpy
entries.description: Debug Python with pdb, breakpoint(), post-mortem inspection, and debugpy remote attach.

entries.name: skill-creator
entries.description: Create, edit, audit, tidy, validate, or restructure AgentSkills and SKILL.md files.

entries.name: spike
entries.description: Run throwaway prototypes to validate feasibility, compare approaches, and report a verdict.

entries.name: taskflow
entries.description: Coordinate multi-step detached tasks as one durable TaskFlow job with owner context, state, waits, and child tasks.

entries.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
entries.description: Example TaskFlow pattern for inbox triage, intent routing, waiting on replies, and later summaries.

entries.name: weather
entries.description: Current weather and forecasts with web_fetch, falling back to wttr.in curl for locations, rain, temperature, travel planning.

entries.name: xurl
entries.description: A Twitter research and content intelligence skill focused on attracting WordPress and Shopify clients. Use to analyze Twitter profiles, threads, and conversations for: (1) Identifying what small agency founders and eCommerce brands are discussing; (2) Understanding pain points around WordPress performance, Shopify CRO, and development bottlenecks; (3) Extracting high-performing content angles; (4) Turning insights into authority-building posts; (5) Converting Twitter intelligence into business leverage for clear content angles, strong positioning, and qualified inbound leads.

data.prompt:
@dinnercrew_ainchors_bot I like the names

- Dinner Crew
- Breaking Bread

In terms of food:

Iconic Melbourne eateries.
Different cuisines that are highly rated.
Not fine dining.
just good food in CBD location.

messages.content: Foodie?

content.text:
Ken! 👋

Yeah, foodie agent — I can help with restaurant recs, menus, cuisines, that sort of thing. What are we thinking?

messages.content: Perfect. Our group is now setup. You have Nick in the group as well

content.text:
Hey Nick! 👋 Welcome to the Dinner Crew 🍽️

You two are the founding members so far. What kind of food are we into? I can start scoping out options whenever we're ready to plan a feed.

messages.content: You have a kickoff - discovery task for us first isn't it?

content.text:
You know me well. Quick discovery round for the Dinner Crew:

**1. Cuisine vibe?** — Any preferences or dealbreakers? (Asian, Italian, Modern Aussie, something specific?)

**2. Location?** — Melbourne CBD? Suburbs? Open to travel?

**3. Budget?** — Casual eats, mid-range, or splurge territory?

**4. Occasion?** — Just a regular catch-up, or celebrating something?

Nick, feel free to chime in with your thoughts too!

messages.content:
Hey. What about the crew name and the below questions that I parked earlier - for the group?

Now, the fun part. Tell me about you and the crew:

• How many of you? Just a regular dinner duo, or is it a squad?

Cuisine leanings? Any no-gos? (Seafood only? Love a good steak? Open to anything?)

• Vibe preference? Hidden laneway gems, white-tablecloth, noisy wine bars, bistros with paper tablecloths?

Budget range? Casual Tuesday or blowout Saturday?

Any recent wins/losses? Any Melbourne restaurants you've loved or hated lately - gives me a compass

content.text:
Good catch — couple of things to run by the group:

**1. Crew name** — Ken floated "Dinner Crew" as the group name, but it was a placeholder. Other ideas? "Melb Supper Club", "The Forking Crew", "Feed Me Club", something else? Or stick with Dinner Crew?

**2. The questions Ken parked earlier:**

- **Headcount** — just us two so far, or are there more coming?
- **Cuisine** — any hard no's? Ken, any preferences on your end, Nick?
- **Vibe** — hidden laneway, white tablecloth, noisy wine bar, casual bistro?
- **Budget** — casual Tuesday or blowout Saturday territory?
- **Recent hits/misses** — any Melbourne restaurants you've loved or hated lately to give me a compass?

Nick, keen to hear your thoughts when you're around!

messages.content: First off, crew is still incomplete. We're still missing Damo, he refuses to install and use telegram. That's a blocker I'm still trying to figure out.

content.text:
Right, Damo's a holdout classic 😅

Couple of ways to handle it:

1. **Bridge him in** — I can't do SMS bridging directly, but you could forward/SS him screenshots of the convo when decisions need his input. Low-fi but works.

2. **He just needs to be in the loop for decisions, not every meme** — we could pick restaurants as a group here, then you relay the final shortlist to him via SMS/WhatsApp for a thumbs up before booking.

3. **The nuclear option** — tell him the crew name is "Damo's Not On Telegram Club" until he joins 😂

What's his objection? Privacy thing, another app fatigue, or just being difficult?

messages.content: App fatigue and his taking a stand. Hahaha

content.text:
Haha the principled stand against yet another messaging app. Classic Damo.

I'd say **option 2** is the pragmatic play — we do the research and shortlist here, then you toss him the final 2-3 choices over SMS for a quick 👍 before locking in a booking. He's not in the chatter, but he's in the decisions.

When we get to booking time I can also check availability/phone numbers for whatever spot we land on so the SMS to Damo has all the details ready to go.

Now — while we wait for Damo to come around (any year now)... you two wanna start answering those discovery questions so I can start hunting?

messages.content: We'll the trio of us have had 3 dinner sessions in the past 3 months. Once a month we try to arrange and catch up. All 3 times have been at Squires Albert Park

content.text:
Nice, so the tradition is already rolling — once a month, the three musketeers. Squires Albert Park three times in a row though 😂 time to break the cycle?

Let me get the ball rolling. I'll look up Squires for context on what you guys like, then scout some alternatives in a similar vein but different enough to keep it interesting.
